title: No apparent way to run commands in a Buildah container using the Kata runtime when SELinux is enabled
body:
**Description**


There doesn't seem to be any way (using Buildah/Podman) to run commands in a Buildah container using Kata as a runtime.

* `buildah --runtime=kata run` results in an error (see https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/2524).
* A workaround suggested on that bug, `buildah mount` then `podman run --rootfs`, fails when SELinux is enabled (SELinux support was re-enabled in Fedora's kata-runtime package since my comments on the bug), because the `buildah mount` (EDIT: or `podman [--runtime=kata] mount`) mounts the FS with a SELinux context category pair (presumably to limit access to the FS from arbitrary running containers), and the process for `kata run` has a different category pair, so cannot access the mounted FS:

  ```
  $ mnt_dir="$(sudo buildah mount <buildah-container>)"

  $ mount | grep merged
  overlay on /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/<id>/merged type overlay (rw,nodev,relatime,context="system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c189,c695",lowerdir=/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/<other-id>,upperdir=/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/<id>/diff,workdir=/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/<id>/work,metacopy=on)

  $ sudo podman --runtime=kata run --rm --rootfs "$mnt_dir" ls
  Error: container create failed (no logs from conmon): EOF

  $ journalctl -e | grep AVC
  Jan 05 22:38:43 <hostname> audit[39194]: AVC avc:  denied  { search } for  pid=39194 comm="pool" name="/" dev="overlay" ino=114527 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_kvm_t:s0:c393,c705 tcontext=system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c189,c695 tclass=dir permissive=0
  ```

  Note the non-matching category pairs for the source and target contexts in the AVC denial message at the end: `c395,c705` and `c189,c695` respectively.

If I manually mount the container's root with the same options as Buildah uses, but without categories in the SELinux context, it's possible to access the root FS with Podman/Kata:

```
$ sudo mount -t overlay overlay -o nodev,metacopy=on,lowerdir=/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/<other-id>,upperdir=/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/<id>/diff,workdir=/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/<id>/work,context='"system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0"' /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/<id>/merged

$ sudo podman --runtime=kata run --rm --rootfs /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/<id>/merged ls
bin
dev
(etc.)
```


**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
buildah-1.18.0-1.fc32.x86_64
```

comments.body:
So it seems like manually specifying the right (i.e. matching) SELinux context categories with `--security-opt` makes things work:

```
mnt_dir="$(sudo buildah mount <buildah-container>)"
sudo podman --runtime=kata run --rm --rootfs --security-opt=label=level:"$(sudo secon -cf "$mnt_dir")" "$mnt_dir" ls
```

Should this maybe be a Podman bug then, to determine and match SELinux context categories as necessary when using `--rootfs`?
So you are suggesting that podman when using a rootfs with SELinux should check the label of the mount point and then set the SELinux label appropriate.

I guess we could check if the rootfs has system_r:system_u:container_file_t:* as a prefix, then use the level.  Not the cleanest solution in the world, but would make users life easier, even if this is a relatively rare corner case.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

This would probably work.

```
$ ctr=$(buildah from alpine)
$ mnt=$(buildah mount $ctr)
$ level=$(secon -l --file $mnt)
$ echo $level
s0:c449,c791
$ podman --runtime=kata run --rm --rootfs $mnt --security-opt label=level:${level} ls
```
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since I gave you a way to do this, I am going to close this issue.
Thanks, haven't had a chance to test yet but if there's a problem using that workaround I'll reopen.
